terms.definition: Beats Per Minute - the tempo of your music. Circuit Tracks supports 40-240 BPM.
A sequence of patterns that play in order, creating a song structure.
MIDI timing signal that keeps multiple devices in sync. Circuit Tracks can send or receive clock.
Novation's free software for deep editing, patch creation, sample management, and backup.
The frequency at which a filter begins to reduce volume. Controls the brightness of a sound.
An effect that creates echo repeats of the audio signal. Circuit Tracks has one global delay.
Audio processing that modifies sound. Circuit Tracks has delay and reverb.
Removes frequencies from a sound. The Filter knob controls the cutoff frequency.
The duration a note is held. A short gate creates staccato; a long gate creates sustained notes.
An all-in-one music production device combining synthesizers, samplers, sequencer, and effects.
A clickable region on a diagram that provides information about a control or feature.
A knob that controls multiple parameters at once. Each patch has custom macro assignments.
Musical Instrument Digital Interface - a protocol for sending note and control data between devices.
One of 16 channels for MIDI data. Synth 1 uses channel 1, Synth 2 uses channel 2, drums use channel 10.
A saved synth sound with all its parameter settings. Also called a preset.
A sequence of notes and steps for all tracks. Up to 32 steps long. Sessions contain 8 patterns.
The ability to play multiple notes simultaneously. Circuit Tracks synths have 6-voice polyphony.
The top-level storage unit containing 8 sessions. Circuit Tracks stores up to 256 projects.
Aligning notes to the grid. Recorded notes are automatically quantized to the nearest step.
Emphasis at the filter cutoff frequency. High resonance creates a whistling or ringing effect.
An effect that simulates room acoustics, adding space and depth to sounds.
A recorded audio file. Circuit Tracks' four sample tracks play back samples for drums and sounds.
One of four tracks (Drum 1-4) that play back audio samples, typically used for drums.
The amount of a track's signal routed to an effect. Each track has independent delay and reverb sends.
A collection of 8 patterns plus all track settings. Think of it as one 'song' or 'scene'.
One position in the sequence. Each pattern has up to 32 steps per track.
A timing offset that adds groove by delaying every other step slightly.
One of two synthesizer tracks (Synth 1-2) that generate sound using the built-in synth engine.
The speed of the music, measured in BPM. Tap the Tempo button or hold it and turn Macro 1.
Tip-Ring-Sleeve - a 3.5mm connector type used for Circuit Tracks' MIDI ports. Requires Type-A adapters.
How hard a note is played. Affects volume and often brightness. Range: 1-127.
